title: quercus rubra var. muehlenbergii
intro: Quercus rubra var. muehlenbergii, also known as the Northern Red Oak, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, specifically in the northeastern and north-central regions. The bark of Quercus rubra var. muehlenbergii contains tannins, which have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Quercus rubra var. muehlenbergii include reducing fever, relieving diarrhea, and supporting the immune system. Quercus rubra var. muehlenbergii is often used in herbal preparations such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in the production of oak bark powder, which can be used to treat various health issues.
